summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the main difference between a linear model and an exponential model?

The key difference is that linear models add a constant amount each period (constant rate of change) while exponential models multiply by a constant factor each period (constant percent change).

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: What does 'multiplicative growth' mean?

Multiplicative growth means a quantity is multiplied by the same factor each period, producing a curved graph that accelerates. This is exponential growth.

Example: A pond's algae coverage doubles every 3 days (exponential). A cleanup crew removes 100 square feet of algae per day (linear removal). If the pond starts with 50 sq ft of algae, can the crew keep up?

Exponential growth always eventually outpaces linear processes. While 100 sq ft/day initially exceeds the algae growth, the doubling will eventually produce more algae than the crew can remove.
